refactor: campaigns are part of radical plan escalation sequence

After N consecutive rejections (default 3), escalation runs in order:
  Step 1: 🛡️ Reliability campaign (tests/proofs)
  Step 2: 🏗️ Modularity campaign (refactoring)
  Step 3: 🚨 Radical plan (rethink from goal.md + failure history)

Previously these were 3 independent triggers. Now they're a single
escalation sequence — campaigns run first to stabilize the codebase,
radical plan only fires if campaigns weren't enough.

fix: lock_timeout=1 (1ms) to actually fail immediately on DDL contention

PostgreSQL lock_timeout='0' DISABLES the timeout (waits forever),
which is the default behavior — exactly what we were trying to avoid.
The correct value is lock_timeout=1 (1ms) which causes the DDL to fail
immediately if ACCESS EXCLUSIVE is unavailable, preventing the cascade
stall pattern where queued DDL blocks all subsequent INSERTs.

Replace python-gitlab sync wrapper (asyncio.to_thread on every call)
with native async httpx client. All 14 API endpoints rewritten.

Key changes:
- GitLabClient uses httpx.AsyncClient with PRIVATE-TOKEN header
- _request() retries 429/5xx/TransportError for GET only (safe retry)
- Respects Retry-After header on 429 responses
- _paginate() handles GitLab page/per_page pagination
- aclose() + async context manager for proper lifecycle
- New create_mr_discussion() method for inline diff comments
- diff_refs null retry uses await asyncio.sleep (not time.sleep)
- resolve_project() URL-encodes path strings
- credential_registry._fetch_identity() uses httpx GET /api/v4/user

Note: python-gitlab remains as dependency — orchestrator, comment_poster,
and discussion_orchestrator still use it directly. Those modules will be
migrated in a follow-up PR to complete the python-gitlab removal.

Closes #365 (partial — client layer done, consumer migration follows)

commit.message:
Fix _align_indentation: handle first-line-correct-rest-at-column-0 pattern

LLMs often indent the first replacement line correctly (matching the
file) but leave subsequent lines at column 0 after newline expansion.
The old algorithm only compared first lines, missing this case entirely.

New algorithm handles two cases:
- Case A: all lines under-indented → add delta to ALL lines
- Case B: first line correct, rest wrong → add delta to subsequent only

Preserves relative indentation within the replacement block.
Added 2 new test cases including real QA scenario. 254 tests pass.

* Make run-java-benchmarks.sh the single source of truth for JMH settings

Remove @Fork, @Warmup, @Measurement annotations from all benchmark classes
so there are no hidden overrides. The script now controls all settings:

- Default mode: publication-quality (5 forks, 5×10s warmup, 5×10s measurement)

but annotations produced quick-iteration-quality results (1 fork, 1s iters).
Now the default run is always safe for publication.

commit.message:
fix(#120,#121): restrict episodic RAG to current conversation + fix accessCount update (#123)

* fix(#120,#121): restrict episodic RAG to current conversation + fix accessCount update

Bug #120 — Episodic memories from other conversations leaking in
  RagRepository.getRelevantContext() now accepts a required `conversationId`
  parameter. After fetching MessageEmbeddingEntity rows from the vector store,
  the list is filtered to only entries whose conversationId matches, so
  memories from an unrelated prior conversation (e.g. a birthday chat) can
  never be injected into the current penguin conversation even when their
  embedding distance is below the 0.4 threshold.
  ChatViewModel.sendMessage() passes `convId` as the new argument.
  All five RagRepositoryTest call-sites updated to supply conversationId.

Bug #121 — accessCount stuck at 0 on the Memory screen
  Investigation showed the SQLite column names are camelCase (accessCount,
  lastAccessedAt) — Room does NOT auto-convert to snake_case. The existing
  @Query UPDATE SQL was already syntactically correct; the root cause was that
  Room 2.7.1's @Query UPDATE path does not reliably notify InvalidationTracker,
  so observeAll() Flow never re-emitted after a stat increment.
  Fix: replace the raw @Query UPDATE batch in MemoryRepositoryImpl with a new
  @Update-annotated CoreMemoryDao.updateAllEntities() method. Room's @Update
  annotation guarantees the InvalidationTracker fires and the Memory screen
  Flow re-emits immediately with the correct accessCount.

Closes #120
Closes #121

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#120,#121): address code review — SQL-level conversationId filter + @Transaction for accessCount

- MessageEmbeddingDao: add getByRowIdsForConversation() pushing conversationId
  predicate into SQL, eliminating post-hoc in-memory filter that caused topK
  starvation when other conversations dominated the vector search results
- RagRepository: use getByRowIdsForConversation() instead of getByRowIds() +
  .filter { it.conversationId == conversationId }
- CoreMemoryDao: add @Transaction incrementAccessStatsAndNotify() wrapping
  updateAccessStatsBatch(); guarantees atomicity (no concurrent read-modify-write
  races) and InvalidationTracker notification on commit so observeAll() re-emits
- MemoryRepositoryImpl: replace updateAllEntities entity-copy approach with
  incrementAccessStatsAndNotify for correct concurrent accessCount increments
- RagRepositoryTest: update existing mocks to getByRowIdsForConversation;
  add cross-conversation isolation test to guard against topK starvation regression

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: nonce type coercion bug in parser strict mode across all runtimes

When randomBytes/token_hex generates an all-digit hex nonce (e.g.
'1234567890123456'), parseAttrs type coercion converts it to a number.
The subsequent strict comparison (number !== string) always fails,
causing a spurious 'Nonce mismatch' error.

Fix: compare nonces as strings in buildMessage/build_message across
Python, TypeScript, and Rust parsers.

Probability of all-digit 16-char hex: (10/16)^16 ≈ 0.01% — matches
the observed flaky CI failure rate.

commit.message:
Agent: Bug Fix (#8354)

* Agent: Bug Fix

* fix: correct test-int-strings fixture assertion type from idempotent to transform

The test-int-strings integration fixture was misconfigured with
"assertion": "idempotent" in its gmloop.json, but contained an
expected.gml file with different content from input.gml. This caused
the fixture-runner discovery validation to throw, blocking the entire
cross-module integration test suite from running (0 of 22 tests).

Changes:
- gmloop.json: Changed assertion from "idempotent" to "transform"
- expected.gml: Updated to match current lint+format pipeline output

The previous expected.gml contained semantically incorrect output
(template interpolation syntax {fps} inside a plain string without
the $ prefix), which would not produce the same runtime behavior as
the original input. The updated expected.gml uses the correct $"..."
template string syntax.

After this fix, 12 integration tests now pass (including test-int-strings),
and 11 other pre-existing fixture mismatches are now individually visible
instead of being masked by the discovery crash.

commit.message:
fix: non-blocking partition DDL to prevent INSERT cascade stalls

Use SET LOCAL lock_timeout='0' in ensure_partition() so the CREATE TABLE
PARTITION OF command never queues an ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock when concurrent
INSERTs are active.

Previously, any busy CZDS/CertStream worker holding a ROW EXCLUSIVE lock
(e.g. a long-running trigram similarity scan) would cause the DDL to queue
indefinitely, blocking *all* subsequent INSERT batches for the parent domain
table until the DDL completed or was manually cancelled.

With lock_timeout='0':
- DDL fails immediately if lock unavailable (no queue, no cascade)
- Session is rolled back cleanly (fixes InFailedSqlTransaction on advisory unlock)
- Re-checks pg_class after rollback: if another process created the partition
  concurrently, proceeds normally
- TLD is retried on the next ingest cycle (acceptable behaviour)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: expand masking controls, coverage, and browser QA guards

- add masking settings UI and dedicated help page
- add masking rules API and runtime rule management improvements
- apply masking across replay/artifact URLs and related templates
- add optional SQL output masking toggle for NLQ/chart responses
- add client-side DOM masking utility for screenshot workflows
- expand unit/integration tests for masking coverage
- enforce Playwright console/page error checks with explicit allow markers

* fix: harden masking toggle and preserve replay/artifact URL actions

- Fix SQL output masking toggle parsing for browser forms that submit both
  hidden and checkbox values for the same field name.
- Keep replay/artifact action URLs functional in the UI while retaining masked
  labels and visible summaries.
- Avoid unnecessary masking rule reconfiguration by caching effective custom
  rule signatures before rebuilding runtime rules.
- Update app and integration tests to cover the toggle semantics and
  visibility-vs-action URL behavior.
- Document the new masking settings, SQL output toggle, and URL behavior in
  README.

* feat: add configurable issue/notification masking controls

- Add a default-on mask_output toggle for user-raised GitHub issues and route
  it through agent/issue creation flows.
- Add per-notification-channel mask_output_enabled configuration (default on)
  and apply masking decisions at dispatch time per channel.
- Update notifications settings UI with creation-time masking toggle and
  masked/raw status badges per channel.
- Extend raise-issue UI flows (errors/traces/incident) to send masking intent.
- Add focused tests covering issue masking toggles, notification channel
  masking behavior, and AI page masking regression coverage.

* fix: apply masking to AI page content and raw JSON tab

- Mask conversation/system content rendered on AI transparency page.
- Mask AI raw span-attribute JSON display and clipboard copy payload.

* feat(masking): make output toggle global across surfaces

- add global output masking setting and route: POST /settings/masking/output
- route Jinja mask filter and JSON masking helpers through global-aware wrappers
- apply global toggle to notifications and GitHub issue payload masking paths
- keep SQL output masking as subordinate toggle when global masking is enabled
- update masking settings/help UI copy to document precedence
- add regression tests for global toggle behavior across preview/template/issue/notification flows
- update README masking docs with global toggle and precedence details

* fix(masking): close payload and query leak paths

* refactor: dedupe masking UI flows

* fix(masking): fail closed when values cannot be copied

- make redaction recurse without requiring deepcopy of entire objects
- return masked sentinel (****) for unsupported/non-copyable values
- guard recursive containers to avoid circular traversal leaks
- add regression tests for mask_value/mask_string with deepcopy failures
- move json import to module scope in masking.py

* feat(masking): harden custom regex validation and add client preflight checks

- validate custom masking patterns with safety guardrails before persistence
- enforce JavaScript compatibility checks aligned with DOM masking normalization
- add runtime smoke-test for custom regexes during validation
- add client-side preflight validation with inline error feedback in settings UI
- add tests for nested-quantifier and lookbehind rejection

* fix: align per-issue masking toggle with global masking state

- Wire raise-issue modal toggle effectiveness to global output masking
- Keep per-issue mask toggle disabled when global masking is enabled
- Enable per-issue mask toggle when global masking is disabled
- Update modal helper copy to reflect effective behavior
- Add tests for both enabled/disabled toggle states
- Document the behavior contract in README

the actual DTO type (e.g., HttpCommandExportOptions) instead of a
